title: SACRAMENTO

raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "AB 1106 establishes a one-time grant program for institutions of higher education to incentivize the adoption and implementation of the new PK-3 Early Childhood Education Specialist Credential.",
    "California districts will need to hire between 11,000 and 15,600 transitional kindergarten teachers due to the expansion of transitional kindergarten.",
    "Bilingual educators are in high demand to meet the needs of the increasing number of dual language learning students.",
    "The bill provides state resources to incentivize higher education institutions to adopt the new credential and build pipelines for students into simultaneous early education credentialing and bilingual authorization.",
    "60% of children under age five in California are Dual Language Learners.",
    "There is a demand for more dual language immersion programs in California.",
    "Teachers who leave their preparation program well qualified are more likely to stay in the profession, reducing teacher attrition."
  ]
}
```
